title: androsace chamaejasme var. lehmanniana
intro: Androsace chamaejasme var. lehmanniana, also known as rock jasmine, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of the Himalayas and the Tibetan Plateau. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce stress, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Rock jasmine can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of conditions, including fever, cough, and skin issues.
